decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed due to exceeding the upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since the material property is already at its maximum effective value, the next most impactful lever is to increase the physical size of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat spreading. I will increase the spreader width by approximately 40% (within the 1.5x step limit) to cover more area above the chip and towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width expands the high-conductivity path for lateral heat dissipation, reducing thermal resistance from the chip to the side boundaries.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip peak temperature below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
Improved heat flux towards the left and right Dirichlet boundaries
risk_notes: Widening the spreader brings it closer to the domain edges; ensure x0 + width does not exceed design domain width (1.2m). Current proposal: 0.25 + 0.98 = 1.23, which slightly exceeds 1.2. Must clamp to max allowable width.

Correction: The proposed new width 0.98 with x0=0.25 results in x_end=1.23, violating the domain width of 1.2. I must adjust the new width to fit within the domain or move x0. Given 'move_toward_chip_center' is a secondary strategy, I will simply cap the width to the maximum possible without moving x0 first, or reduce the increment. Max width allowed at x0=0.25 is 1.2 - 0.25 = 0.95. I will set new width to 0.95.
